product_overview: CMTX AX Planner OS is a frontend platform that automates collection and analysis of semiconductor R&D and industry trends. It provides an Automation Center for registering crawling targets, an interactive GR Hub for exploring grants and policies, and a local admin mode for conditional persistence and privileged actions.
core_goals: Register and manage web crawling targets (name, URL, keywords, category) to feed the platform's collection pipeline.
Explore and inspect grants and policy items with on-demand detail panels that include original links and AI summaries.
Switch between guest and admin modes so actions either simulate locally or persist to the Supabase backend (when authenticated).
Create and iterate on AX project planning items to organize R&D initiatives and tasks.

features.name: Admin Authentication (Local)
features.description: Allow a user to authenticate locally using an environment-based admin key to enable admin mode and persistent actions.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open admin login modal -> Enter access key -> Submit authentication -> See admin mode indicated in the sidebar
Navigate to / -> Open admin login modal -> Enter incorrect or empty key -> Submit authentication -> See validation message prompting correct access key

features.name: Crawling Target Management (Automation Center)
features.description: Let users register new crawling targets with name, URL, keywords and category; persistence depends on admin vs guest mode (admin persists to Supabase, guest simulates locally).
user_flows: Navigate to /automation -> Start new target registration -> Provide target name -> Provide target URL -> Provide seed keywords -> Choose target category -> Submit registration -> If authenticated as admin see confirmation that target was saved persistently

Navigate to /automation -> Start new target registration -> Provide target name -> Provide target URL -> Provide seed keywords -> Choose target category -> Submit registration -> If in guest mode see a simulation notice and the target appears for current session only
Navigate to /automation -> Start new target registration -> Provide target name -> Leave URL empty -> Submit registration -> See validation error requesting a valid URL

features.name: Interactive Dashboard (GR Hub)
features.description: Present grant and policy lists as individually selectable items that open a sliding detail panel with source link and AI-generated summary.
user_flows: Navigate to /gr-hub -> Scan grant and policy lists -> Focus an item to preview additional context -> Select an item to open its detail panel -> Read original link and AI summary in the panel -> Close detail panel to return to the list
Navigate to /gr-hub -> Select multiple items in sequence -> Open and read each item's detail panel -> Close each panel after review

features.name: AX Project Planning Center
features.description: Provide a workspace for users to view and create AX project plans and lightweight plan metadata for R&D initiatives.
user_flows: Navigate to /ax-planning -> View the AX planning dashboard overview -> Start a new plan -> Enter plan title and basic details -> Save the plan -> See the new plan listed in the dashboard
Navigate to /ax-planning -> Open an existing plan from the list -> Review plan details and timeline -> Return to the planning dashboard

routes.description: GR Hub overview with interactive cards
Automation Center for managing crawling targets

features.name: Admin Authentication
features.description: Local admin access via environment-based key
user_interactions: See admin status change in sidebar

features.name: Crawling Target Management
features.description: Manage crawling targets with conditional persistence
user_interactions: See simulation alert for guests or real success for admins

features.name: Interactive Dashboard
features.description: Individually clickable items with detail panels
user_interactions: Hover over list items in Grant or Policy cards
Click on a specific list item
View detailed information in sliding side panel

known_limitations.impact: DB targets not yet automatically triggered for collection
